agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics platform introducing automated route optimization for drivers at shift start, with dynamic recalculation triggers, customer proximity notifications, and vehicle-class enforcement. This spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, fleet management, delivery lifecycle, and customer communication.","bounded_context":"This feature touches four bounded contexts: (1) Route Optimization — owns route calculation, recalculation triggers, stop ordering, and ETA computation; (2) Fleet Management — owns driver profiles, vehicle classes, licensing constraints, shift lifecycle; (3) Delivery Management — owns delivery lifecycle (pending, in-progress, failed, completed), zone assignment, delivery attributes (size/weight/class); (4) Customer Notification — owns notification rules, delivery proximity windows, customer communication channels. Each context must communicate via well-defined domain events, not direct coupling.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops assigned to a driver, calculated to minimize traversal cost (distance/time) given current pending deliveries in the driver's zone."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic area used to partition deliveries and assign them to drivers. A driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift."},{"term":"Shift","definition":"A bounded working period for a driver, beginning when the driver starts work and ending when they clock out. Route assignment is triggered at shift start."},{"term":"Recalculation Trigger","definition":"An event that invalidates the current optimized route and forces a new route computation. Defined triggers: new delivery added to zone, delivery marked as failed, driver manual deviation."},{"term":"Manual Deviation","definition":"When a driver departs from the system-suggested route order, visiting stops out of sequence or traveling off the planned path."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"The set of the three upcoming delivery stops on the driver's active route. Customers whose deliveries fall within this window receive a proximity notification."},{"term":"Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of vehicle type (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight) that determines which deliveries a driver is eligible to carry. Drivers are licensed for specific classes."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"The vehicle class(es) a driver is authorized and equipped to operate. Acts as a hard constraint on delivery assignment."},{"term":"Stop Time Estimate","definition":"The expected duration a driver spends at each delivery stop. Defaults to 4 minutes but is overridable by the operations team at the zone level."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery that could not be completed (e.g., recipient unavailable, address inaccessible). Triggers route recalculation to remove the stop and re-optimize remaining stops."},{"term":"Pending Delivery","definition":"A delivery that has been created and is awaiting assignment to a driver's route. Only pending deliveries in a driver's zone are candidates for route assignment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically generate an optimized route from all pending deliveries in the driver's assigned zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational workflow trigger — the optimization algorithm and trigger timing are policy decisions that could change (e.g., routes could be pre-computed before shift start).","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Within the system's processing window of shift start, a route containing all eligible pending deliveries in the driver's zone is created and assigned to the driver."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's active zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Recalculation policy could be changed to batch new deliveries or defer recalculation to avoid disrupting the driver mid-route.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a new delivery is created in a zone with an active driver route, the route is recalculated to include the new delivery within the defined SLA."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a delivery on the active route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy decision — an alternative could be to simply skip the failed stop without full recalculation.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a delivery is marked failed, it is removed from the active route and the remaining stops are re-optimized."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The route must be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Deviation handling is a policy — the system could alternatively flag the deviation without recalculating.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When the system detects a driver has deviated from the planned route sequence, a recalculation is triggered using the driver's current position and remaining deliveries."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on the driver's active route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The notification window size (3 stops) is a configurable business policy, not a physical constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a delivery transitions into the top 3 positions of the active route, exactly one proximity notification is sent to the customer."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that requires a vehicle class they are not licensed for.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a hard safety and regulatory constraint. Assigning a motorcycle driver a freight shipment is physically impossible and potentially illegal. Violating this corrupts the integrity of the assignment.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: For every delivery on a driver's route, delivery.requiredVehicleClass is contained within driver.licensedVehicleClasses. No exceptions."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated time per delivery stop is 4 minutes, overridable by the operations team at the zone level.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 4-minute default and override mechanism are operational policies that can be tuned.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Route ETA calculations use the zone-level override if set; otherwise default to 4 minutes per stop. The override must be set by a user with operations team permissions."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Only pending deliveries within the driver's assigned zone are candidates for route assignment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zone boundaries are a fundamental partitioning constraint. Assigning cross-zone deliveries violates the zone ownership model and could cause overlapping routes between drivers.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Every delivery on a driver's route belongs to the same zone the driver is assigned to for that shift."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are 5 pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"An optimized route containing all 5 deliveries is generated and assigned to the driver, with ETAs calculated using the zone's stop time estimate"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active route with 8 remaining stops","when":"A new delivery is added to the driver's zone","then":"The route is recalculated to include the new delivery, re-optimizing the stop order from the driver's current position"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route and delivery #4 is marked as failed","when":"The failure event is processed","then":"Delivery #4 is removed from the route, remaining stops are re-optimized, and the customer for delivery #4 is NOT in the 'next 3 stops' notification window"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver has an active route with stops A→B→C→D and the driver skips B and goes to C","when":"The system detects the deviation","then":"The route is recalculated from the driver's current position with remaining undelivered stops (including B if not completed)"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A driver is completing stop #5 of 10 on their route","when":"Stop #5 is completed and the driver advances","then":"The customers for stops #6, #7, and #8 (the new 'next 3') are evaluated, and any customer newly entering the window receives exactly one notification"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A motorcycle-licensed driver is active in a zone","when":"A freight-class delivery is added to that zone","then":"The freight delivery is NOT included in the motorcycle driver's route, even during recalculation"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"The operations team has set the zone stop time to 6 minutes for Zone A","when":"A route is calculated for a driver in Zone A with 10 stops","then":"The total estimated route time includes 60 minutes of stop time (10 × 6 min) plus transit time"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are zero pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"The driver is assigned an empty route (or placed in an idle/awaiting state) and will receive a route when the first delivery enters the zone"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A driver has an active route with exactly 3 remaining stops","when":"A new delivery is added and the route recalculates","then":"The next-3-stops window is re-evaluated and any customer newly entering the window is notified; any customer who was in the window but is now beyond stop 3 is NOT sent a cancellation notification"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"Two drivers are active in the same zone with different vehicle classes","when":"A new delivery enters the zone requiring van-class vehicle","then":"Only the van-licensed driver's route is recalculated to include the delivery; the motorcycle driver's route is unaffected"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Driver starts shift with no pending deliveries","expected_behavior":"Driver enters an idle state. When the first delivery arrives in their zone, a route is created. The driver is not stuck — the system must handle the zero-delivery case gracefully."},{"scenario":"All remaining deliveries on a route are marked as failed","expected_behavior":"The driver's route becomes empty. Driver returns to idle/awaiting state. No spurious recalculations triggered."},{"scenario":"Multiple recalculation triggers fire simultaneously (new delivery added + driver deviates at the same moment)","expected_behavior":"The system must coalesce or serialize these triggers to avoid race conditions. Only one recalculation should execute with the latest state, not two competing recalculations."},{"scenario":"Customer receives 'next 3 stops' notification but route recalculation moves them out of the window","expected_behavior":"The notification already sent cannot be unsent. The system should NOT send a retraction. The customer may receive a new notification if they re-enter the window later — but must not receive duplicate notifications for the same delivery."},{"scenario":"Driver deviates but returns to the original route voluntarily","expected_behavior":"Deviation detection fires and route recalculates. If the recalculated route is identical to the original, no visible change to the driver. The system should not penalize temporary deviations."},{"scenario":"A delivery requires a vehicle class and no driver in the zone is licensed for it","expected_behavior":"The delivery remains in pending state unassigned. The system should surface this to operations as an unassignable delivery requiring intervention (e.g., dispatch from another zone)."},{"scenario":"Zone stop time override is changed while drivers have active routes","expected_behavior":"Active routes should recalculate ETAs using the new stop time. Whether this triggers a full re-optimization or just an ETA update needs clarification."},{"scenario":"A driver is the only one in a zone and starts their shift — 50 deliveries are pending","expected_behavior":"All 50 eligible deliveries (matching vehicle class) are assigned to that single driver. The system should not silently drop deliveries, but operations may need capacity warnings."},{"scenario":"Driver completes a delivery that was in another customer's 'next 3' notification","expected_behavior":"The window advances. The customer at position #4 now enters the 'next 3' window and receives a notification."},{"scenario":"New delivery added to zone but driver is on their last stop","expected_behavior":"Route recalculates to add the new delivery after the current stop. The driver's route extends rather than ending."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly impacts fuel costs, driver hours, and deliveries-per-shift throughput. A well-optimized system can increase delivery density by 15-25%. The 4-minute stop time default affects ETA accuracy, which impacts customer satisfaction and retry costs for failed deliveries.","operational":"Dynamic recalculation introduces computational load — every new delivery or deviation triggers re-optimization. Without throttling/coalescing, this could degrade system performance during peak hours. The vehicle class constraint adds assignment complexity but prevents costly mis-deliveries. Operations teams gain zone-level control over stop times, enabling localized tuning.","user":"Drivers get a clear, auto-updating route — reducing cognitive load and decision fatigue. Customers get proactive 'almost there' notifications, improving the delivery experience and reducing 'where is my package' inquiries. However, frequent route recalculations could frustrate drivers if the route keeps changing mid-shift.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Recalculation storms during peak periods degrading performance; (2) Deviation detection false positives causing unnecessary recalculations; (3) Notification spam if customers repeatedly enter/exit the 3-stop window due to recalculations; (4) Vehicle class data being stale or incorrect, leading to assignment violations.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Core workflow change — routes are auto-assigned and dynamically updated. Reduces autonomy but increases efficiency. Frequent recalculations could be disruptive."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive proximity notifications improving delivery predictability. Risk of notification noise if routes recalculate frequently."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Gains zone-level stop time control. Must monitor unassignable deliveries (vehicle class mismatches). Needs visibility into recalculation frequency and route quality."},{"group":"Dispatchers","impact":"Reduced manual route planning burden, but must handle edge cases like cross-zone deliveries and vehicle class gaps."},{"group":"Finance/Management","impact":"Expects measurable improvements in deliveries-per-shift and fuel efficiency. Needs KPIs to validate optimization effectiveness."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec is well-structured for a product description but contains several ambiguities that would lead to divergent implementations. The core workflow (shift start → route → recalculation triggers → notifications) is clear, but critical operational details around deviation detection, optimization objectives, recalculation throttling, and multi-driver zone coordination are unspecified. These are not missing context (someone forgot to mention them) — they are ambiguous because the spec uses terms like 'optimized' and 'deviates' without defining the criteria.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class constraint is a hard invariant — unambiguous and critical","Recalculation trigger list is explicitly enumerated","Customer notification at 'next 3 stops' is clearly defined as a threshold","Zone-scoped stop time override mechanism is well-specified","Delivery-to-zone assignment constraint is clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["How deviation is detected (GPS threshold? Sequence violation? Both?)","What 'optimized' means — shortest distance, shortest time, priority-weighted, or multi-objective","How multi-driver zones coordinate — are deliveries partitioned or shared?","Recalculation throttling/coalescing strategy under high-frequency triggers","Whether notification is idempotent per delivery or per window-entry event","How zone boundaries interact with real-time traffic or geographic obstacles"],"assumptions":["Each driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift","A delivery belongs to exactly one zone","Vehicle class is a property of both the driver and the delivery, not just the vehicle","Optimization runs synchronously enough that the driver receives the route before departing","The 'next 3 stops' window is evaluated from the driver's current position in the route, not from a fixed point"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Optimized route' is undefined — optimized for what objective? Shortest distance, shortest time, delivery priority weighting, or a composite? Different optimization objectives produce fundamentally different routes. A time-optimized route may differ significantly from a distance-optimized one, especially in urban zones with traffic variance.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team","Finance/Management"],"options":["Optimize for shortest total distance (fuel cost focus)","Optimize for shortest total time including traffic estimates (throughput focus)","Optimize for delivery priority weighting — urgent deliveries first (SLA focus)","Multi-objective with configurable weights per zone"],"recommendation":"Define optimization as shortest-time with a priority override mechanism. Time-based optimization aligns with maximizing deliveries-per-shift while allowing operations to boost urgent deliveries."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Manual deviation' detection mechanism is unspecified. This could mean: (a) the driver visits stops out of the suggested sequence, (b) the driver's GPS position diverges from the planned path by a threshold, or (c) the driver explicitly signals a deviation in the app. Each interpretation has vastly different technical and UX implications.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Sequence-based: deviation = completing stops out of suggested order","GPS-based: deviation = driver's position diverges from planned path beyond a distance threshold","Explicit: driver taps 'I am deviating' in the app","Hybrid: sequence-based as primary, with GPS as secondary signal"],"recommendation":"Use sequence-based detection as primary (driver completes or skips a stop out of order). GPS-based deviation is prone to false positives from traffic detours and should only be a secondary signal, if used at all."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-driver zone coordination is not addressed. When multiple drivers are active in the same zone, are pending deliveries partitioned among them (each driver gets a subset) or does each driver's route consider all pending deliveries? This fundamentally changes the optimization problem and affects fairness and efficiency.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team","Dispatchers"],"options":["Partition: deliveries are divided among active drivers at assignment time, each driver owns their subset","Shared pool: all drivers see all deliveries, system dynamically balances","Sequential: first driver gets all deliveries, second driver gets overflow or new arrivals"],"recommendation":"Partition deliveries among active drivers at route creation time, with rebalancing on recalculation triggers. This prevents duplicate assignments and gives drivers clear ownership of their stops."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Recalculation frequency is unbounded. In a high-volume zone, new deliveries could arrive every few seconds, each triggering a recalculation. The spec does not address throttling, debouncing, or batching of recalculation triggers. Without this, the system could enter a recalculation storm that degrades performance and constantly changes the driver's route.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team","Engineering"],"options":["Immediate recalculation on every trigger (spec as written)","Debounced: wait N seconds after last trigger before recalculating","Batched: recalculate at fixed intervals (e.g., every 2 minutes) incorporating all pending changes","Driver-controlled: notify driver of pending changes, let them trigger recalculation"],"recommendation":"Implement debounced recalculation with a 30-second window. Coalesce all triggers within the window into a single recalculation. This balances responsiveness with stability."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

This spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, fleet management, delivery lifecycle, and customer communication.","bounded_context":"This feature touches four bounded contexts: (1) Route Optimization — owns route calculation, recalculation triggers, stop orderi

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this logistics domain scenario carefully.

This is a "Smart Route Optimization" feature. Let me break down the bounded contexts, business rules, and produce a full domain analysis.

Key bounded contexts I see:
1. Route Optimization - route calculation, recalculation triggers
2. Fleet/Driver Management - vehicle class licensing, shift management
3. Delivery Management - delivery lifecycle, zones
4. Customer Notification - next 3 stops window

Let me identify business rules, invariants, edge cases, and any ambiguities.

Potential ambiguities:
- "zone" - what defines a zone? Geographic boundary? How are zones assigned to drivers?


- "manually deviates" - how is deviation detected? GPS threshold?
- "next 3 stops" - is this exactly 3, or could it be configurable?
- "licensed vehicle class" - who maintains this mapping? Where does vehicle class data come from?
- "optimized route" - optimized for what? Distance? Time? Priority?
- "failed delivery" - what constitutes failure? Who marks it?
- "operations team per zone" - override granularity?

These are all areas where the spec describes behavior but leaves room for interpretation. I need to flag these as ambiguities and then work through the full domain analysis.

Needs visibility into recalculation frequency and route quality."},{"group":"Dispatchers","impact":"Reduced manual route planning burden, but must handle edge cases like cross-zone deliveries and vehicle class gaps."},{"group":"Finance/Management","impact":"Expects measurable improvements in deliveries-per-shift and fuel efficiency. Needs KPIs to validate optimization effectiveness."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec is well-structured for a product description but contains several ambiguities that would lead to divergent implementations. The core workflow (shift start → route → recalculation triggers → notifications) is clear, but critical operational details around deviation detection, optimization objectives, recalculation throttling, and multi-driver zone coordination are unspecified. These are not missing context (someone forgot to mention them) — they are ambiguous because the spec uses terms like 'optimized' and 'deviates' without defining the criteria.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class constraint is a hard invariant — unambiguous and critical","Recalculation trigger list is explicitly enumerated","Customer notification at 'next 3 stops' is clearly defined as a threshold","Zone-scoped stop time override mechanism is well-specified","Delivery-to-zone assignment constraint is clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["How deviation is detected (GPS threshold? Sequence violation? Both?)","What 'optimized' means — shortest distance, shortest time, priority-weighted, or multi-objective","How multi-driver zones coordinate — are deliveries partitioned or shared?","Recalculation throttling/coalescing strategy under high-frequency triggers","Whether notification is idempotent per delivery or per window-entry event","How zone boundaries interact with real-time traffic or geographic obstacles"],"assumptions":["Each driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift","A delivery belongs to exactly one zone","Vehicle class is a property of both the driver and the delivery, not just the vehicle","Optimization runs synchronously enough that the driver receives the route before departing","The 'next 3 stops' window is evaluated from the driver's current position in the route, not from a fixed point"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Optimized route' is undefined — optimized for what objective? Shortest distance, shortest time, delivery priority weighting, or a composite? Different optimization objectives produce fundamentally different routes. A time-optimized route may differ significantly from a distance-optimized one, especially in urban zones with traffic variance.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team","Finance/Management"],"options":["Optimize for shortest total distance (fuel cost focus)","Optimize for shortest total time including traffic estimates (throughput focus)","Optimize for delivery priority weighting — urgent deliveries first (SLA focus)","Multi-objective with configurable weights per zone"],"recommendation":"Define optimization as shortest-time with a priority override mechanism. Time-based optimization aligns with maximizing deliveries-per-shift while allowing operations to boost urgent deliveries."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Manual deviation' detection mechanism is unspecified. This could mean: (a) the driver visits stops out of the suggested sequence, (b) the driver's GPS position diverges from the planned path by a threshold, or (c) the driver explicitly signals a deviation in the app. Each interpretation has vastly different technical and UX implications.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Sequence-based: deviation = completing stops out of suggested order","GPS-based: deviation = driver's position diverges from planned path beyond a distance threshold","Explicit: driver taps 'I am deviating' in the app","Hybrid: sequence-based as primary, with GPS as secondary signal"],"recommendation":"Use sequence-based detection as primary (driver completes or skips a stop out of order). GPS-based deviation is prone to false positives from traffic detours and should only be a secondary signal, if used at all."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-driver zone coordination is not addressed. When multiple drivers are active in the same zone, are pending deliveries partitioned among them (each driver gets a subset) or does each driver's route consider all pending deliveries? This fundamentally changes the optimization problem and affects fairness and efficiency.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team","Dispatchers"],"options":["Partition: deliveries are divided among active drivers at assignment time, each driver owns their subset","Shared pool: all drivers see all deliveries, system dynamically balances","Sequential: first driver gets all deliveries, second driver gets overflow or new arrivals"],"recommendation":"Partition deliveries among active drivers at route creation time, with rebalancing on recalculation triggers. This prevents duplicate assignments and gives drivers clear ownership of their stops."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Recalculation frequency is unbounded. In a high-volume zone, new deliveries could arrive every few seconds, each triggering a recalculation. The spec does not address throttling, debouncing, or batching of recalculation triggers. Without this, the system could enter a recalculation storm that degrades performance and constantly changes the driver's route.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team","Engineering"],"options":["Immediate recalculation on every trigger (spec as written)","Debounced: wait N seconds after last trigger before recalculating","Batched: recalculate at fixed intervals (e.g., every 2 minutes) incorporating all pending changes","Driver-controlled: notify driver of pending changes, let them trigger recalculation"],"recommendation":"Implement debounced recalculation with a 30-second window. Coalesce all triggers within the window into a single recalculation. This balances responsiveness with stability."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

trace.result: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics platform introducing automated route optimization for drivers at shift start, with dynamic recalculation triggers, customer proximity notifications, and vehicle-class enforcement. This spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, fleet management, delivery lifecycle, and customer communication.","bounded_context":"This feature touches four bounded contexts: (1) Route Optimization — owns route calculation, recalculation triggers, stop ordering, and ETA computation; (2) Fleet Management — owns driver profiles, vehicle classes, licensing constraints, shift lifecycle; (3) Delivery Management — owns delivery lifecycle (pending, in-progress, failed, completed), zone assignment, delivery attributes (size/weight/class); (4) Customer Notification — owns notification rules, delivery proximity windows, customer communication channels. Each context must communicate via well-defined domain events, not direct coupling.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops assigned to a driver, calculated to minimize traversal cost (distance/time) given current pending deliveries in the driver's zone."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic area used to partition deliveries and assign them to drivers. A driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift."},{"term":"Shift","definition":"A bounded working period for a driver, beginning when the driver starts work and ending when they clock out. Route assignment is triggered at shift start."},{"term":"Recalculation Trigger","definition":"An event that invalidates the current optimized route and forces a new route computation. Defined triggers: new delivery added to zone, delivery marked as failed, driver manual deviation."},{"term":"Manual Deviation","definition":"When a driver departs from the system-suggested route order, visiting stops out of sequence or traveling off the planned path."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"The set of the three upcoming delivery stops on the driver's active route. Customers whose deliveries fall within this window receive a proximity notification."},{"term":"Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of vehicle type (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight) that determines which deliveries a driver is eligible to carry. Drivers are licensed for specific classes."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"The vehicle class(es) a driver is authorized and equipped to operate. Acts as a hard constraint on delivery assignment."},{"term":"Stop Time Estimate","definition":"The expected duration a driver spends at each delivery stop. Defaults to 4 minutes but is overridable by the operations team at the zone level."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery that could not be completed (e.g., recipient unavailable, address inaccessible). Triggers route recalculation to remove the stop and re-optimize remaining stops."},{"term":"Pending Delivery","definition":"A delivery that has been created and is awaiting assignment to a driver's route. Only pending deliveries in a driver's zone are candidates for route assignment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically generate an optimized route from all pending deliveries in the driver's assigned zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational workflow trigger — the optimization algorithm and trigger timing are policy decisions that could change (e.g., routes could be pre-computed before shift start).","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Within the system's processing window of shift start, a route containing all eligible pending deliveries in the driver's zone is created and assigned to the driver."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's active zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Recalculation policy could be changed to batch new deliveries or defer recalculation to avoid disrupting the driver mid-route.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a new delivery is created in a zone with an active driver route, the route is recalculated to include the new delivery within the defined SLA."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a delivery on the active route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy decision — an alternative could be to simply skip the failed stop without full recalculation.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a delivery is marked failed, it is removed from the active route and the remaining stops are re-optimized."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The route must be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Deviation handling is a policy — the system could alternatively flag the deviation without recalculating.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When the system detects a driver has deviated from the planned route sequence, a recalculation is triggered using the driver's current position and remaining deliveries."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on the driver's active route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The notification window size (3 stops) is a configurable business policy, not a physical constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a delivery transitions into the top 3 positions of the active route, exactly one proximity notification is sent to the customer."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that requires a vehicle class they are not licensed for.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a hard safety and regulatory constraint. Assigning a motorcycle driver a freight shipment is physically impossible and potentially illegal. Violating this corrupts the integrity of the assignment.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: For every delivery on a driver's route, delivery.requiredVehicleClass is contained within driver.licensedVehicleClasses. No exceptions."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated time per delivery stop is 4 minutes, overridable by the operations team at the zone level.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 4-minute default and override mechanism are operational policies that can be tuned.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Route ETA calculations use the zone-level override if set; otherwise default to 4 minutes per stop. The override must be set by a user with operations team permissions."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Only pending deliveries within the driver's assigned zone are candidates for route assignment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zone boundaries are a fundamental partitioning constraint. Assigning cross-zone deliveries violates the zone ownership model and could cause overlapping routes between drivers.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Every delivery on a driver's route belongs to the same zone the driver is assigned to for that shift."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are 5 pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"An optimized route containing all 5 deliveries is generated and assigned to the driver, with ETAs calculated using the zone's stop time estimate"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active route with 8 remaining stops","when":"A new delivery is added to the driver's zone","then":"The route is recalculated to include the new delivery, re-optimizing the stop order from the driver's current position"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route and delivery #4 is marked as failed","when":"The failure event is processed","then":"Delivery #4 is removed from the route, remaining stops are re-optimized, and the customer for delivery #4 is NOT in the 'next 3 stops' notification window"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver has an active route with stops A→B→C→D and the driver skips B and goes to C","when":"The system detects the deviation","then":"The route is recalculated from the driver's current position with remaining undelivered stops (including B if not completed)"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A driver is completing stop #5 of 10 on their route","when":"Stop #5 is completed and the driver advances","then":"The customers for stops #6, #7, and #8 (the new 'next 3') are evaluated, and any customer newly entering the window receives exactly one notification"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A motorcycle-licensed driver is active in a zone","when":"A freight-class delivery is added to that zone","then":"The freight delivery is NOT included in the motorcycle driver's route, even during recalculation"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"The operations team has set the zone stop time to 6 minutes for Zone A","when":"A route is calculated for a driver in Zone A with 10 stops","then":"The total estimated route time includes 60 minutes of stop time (10 × 6 min) plus transit time"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are zero pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"The driver is assigned an empty route (or placed in an idle/awaiting state) and will receive a route when the first delivery enters the zone"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A driver has an active route with exactly 3 remaining stops","when":"A new delivery is added and the route recalculates","then":"The next-3-stops window is re-evaluated and any customer newly entering the window is notified; any customer who was in the window but is now beyond stop 3 is NOT sent a cancellation notification"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"Two drivers are active in the same zone with different vehicle classes","when":"A new delivery enters the zone requiring van-class vehicle","then":"Only the van-licensed driver's route is recalculated to include the delivery; the motorcycle driver's route is unaffected"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Driver starts shift with no pending deliveries","expected_behavior":"Driver enters an idle state. When the first delivery arrives in their zone, a route is created. The driver is not stuck — the system must handle the zero-delivery case gracefully."},{"scenario":"All remaining deliveries on a route are marked as failed","expected_behavior":"The driver's route becomes empty. Driver returns to idle/awaiting state. No spurious recalculations triggered."},{"scenario":"Multiple recalculation triggers fire simultaneously (new delivery added + driver deviates at the same moment)","expected_behavior":"The system must coalesce or serialize these triggers to avoid race conditions. Only one recalculation should execute with the latest state, not two competing recalculations."},{"scenario":"Customer receives 'next 3 stops' notification but route recalculation moves them out of the window","expected_behavior":"The notification already sent cannot be unsent. The system should NOT send a retraction. The customer may receive a new notification if they re-enter the window later — but must not receive duplicate notifications for the same delivery."},{"scenario":"Driver deviates but returns to the original route voluntarily","expected_behavior":"Deviation detection fires and route recalculates. If the recalculated route is identical to the original, no visible change to the driver. The system should not penalize temporary deviations."},{"scenario":"A delivery requires a vehicle class and no driver in the zone is licensed for it","expected_behavior":"The delivery remains in pending state unassigned. The system should surface this to operations as an unassignable delivery requiring intervention (e.g., dispatch from another zone)."},{"scenario":"Zone stop time override is changed while drivers have active routes","expected_behavior":"Active routes should recalculate ETAs using the new stop time. Whether this triggers a full re-optimization or just an ETA update needs clarification."},{"scenario":"A driver is the only one in a zone and starts their shift — 50 deliveries are pending","expected_behavior":"All 50 eligible deliveries (matching vehicle class) are assigned to that single driver. The system should not silently drop deliveries, but operations may need capacity warnings."},{"scenario":"Driver completes a delivery that was in another customer's 'next 3' notification","expected_behavior":"The window advances. The customer at position #4 now enters the 'next 3' window and receives a notification."},{"scenario":"New delivery added to zone but driver is on their last stop","expected_behavior":"Route recalculates to add the new delivery after the current stop. The driver's route extends rather than ending."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly impacts fuel costs, driver hours, and deliveries-per-shift throughput. A well-optimized system can increase delivery density by 15-25%. The 4-minute stop time default affects ETA accuracy, which impacts customer satisfaction and retry costs for failed deliveries.","operational":"Dynamic recalculation introduces computational load — every new delivery or deviation triggers re-optimization. Without throttling/coalescing, this could degrade system performance during peak hours. The vehicle class constraint adds assignment complexity but prevents costly mis-deliveries. Operations teams gain zone-level control over stop times, enabling localized tuning.","user":"Drivers get a clear, auto-updating route — reducing cognitive load and decision fatigue. Customers get proactive 'almost there' notifications, improving the delivery experience and reducing 'where is my package' inquiries. However, frequent route recalculations could frustrate drivers if the route keeps changing mid-shift.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Recalculation storms during peak periods degrading performance; (2) Deviation detection false positives causing unnecessary recalculations; (3) Notification spam if customers repeatedly enter/exit the 3-stop window due to recalculations; (4) Vehicle class data being stale or incorrect, leading to assignment violations.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Core workflow change — routes are auto-assigned and dynamically updated. Reduces autonomy but increases efficiency. Frequent recalculations could be disruptive."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive proximity notifications improving delivery predictability. Risk of notification noise if routes recalculate frequently."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Gains zone-level stop time control. Must monitor unassignable deliveries (vehicle class mismatches). Needs visibility into recalculation frequency and route quality."},{"group":"Dispatchers","impact":"Reduced manual route planning burden, but must handle edge cases like cross-zone deliveries and vehicle class gaps."},{"group":"Finance/Management","impact":"Expects measurable improvements in deliveries-per-shift and fuel efficiency. Needs KPIs to validate optimization effectiveness."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec is well-structured for a product description but contains several ambiguities that would lead to divergent implementations. The core workflow (shift start → route → recalculation triggers → notifications) is clear, but critical operational details around deviation detection, optimization objectives, recalculation throttling, and multi-driver zone coordination are unspecified. These are not missing context (someone forgot to mention them) — they are ambiguous because the spec uses terms like 'optimized' and 'deviates' without defining the criteria.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class constraint is a hard invariant — unambiguous and critical","Recalculation trigger list is explicitly enumerated","Customer notification at 'next 3 stops' is clearly defined as a threshold","Zone-scoped stop time override mechanism is well-specified","Delivery-to-zone assignment constraint is clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["How deviation is detected (GPS threshold? Sequence violation? Both?)","What 'optimized' means — shortest distance, shortest time, priority-weighted, or multi-objective","How multi-driver zones coordinate — are deliveries partitioned or shared?","Recalculation throttling/coalescing strategy under high-frequency triggers","Whether notification is idempotent per delivery or per window-entry event","How zone boundaries interact with real-time traffic or geographic obstacles"],"assumptions":["Each driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift","A delivery belongs to exactly one zone","Vehicle class is a property of both the driver and the delivery, not just the vehicle","Optimization runs synchronously enough that the driver receives the route before departing","The 'next 3 stops' window is evaluated from the driver's current position in the route, not from a fixed point"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Optimized route' is undefined — optimized for what objective? A time-optimized route may differ significantly from a distance-optimized one, especially in urban zones with traffic variance.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team","Finance/Management"],"options":["Optimize for shortest total distance (fuel cost focus)","Optimize for shortest total time including traffic estimates (throughput focus)","Optimize for delivery priority weighting — urgent deliveries first (SLA focus)","Multi-objective with configurable weights per zone"],"recommendation":"Define optimization as shortest-time with a priority override mechanism. This could mean: (a) the driver visits stops out of the suggested sequence, (b) the driver's GPS position diverges from the planned path by a threshold, or (c) the driver explicitly signals a deviation in the app. Each interpretation has vastly different technical and UX implications.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Sequence-based: deviation = completing stops out of suggested order","GPS-based: deviation = driver's position diverges from planned path beyond a distance threshold","Explicit: driver taps 'I am deviating' in the app","Hybrid: sequence-based as primary, with GPS as secondary signal"],"recommendation":"Use sequence-based detection as primary (driver completes or skips a stop out of order). GPS-based deviation is prone to false positives from traffic detours and should only be a secondary signal, if used at all."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-driver zone coordination is not addressed.
